task_completion.description: Select the 'gdp' variable in the Gretl main window
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'gdp' variable should be highlighted or selected in the main Gretl dataset view.

task_completion.description: Generate and display the time series plot for GDP
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph window is open showing a time series line plot. The x-axis should represent time (spanning roughly 1984 to 2009) and the y-axis should represent GDP values.

task_completion.description: Open the save or export dialog for the graph
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save as' or 'Export' dialog box is open over the graph window, indicating the agent is attempting to save the plot.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the correct output directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog shows the current directory path as '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output'.

task_completion.description: Save the file as a PNG with the correct filename
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename input field in the save dialog contains 'gdp_time_series.png' with the PNG format selected, OR a file manager/terminal window shows the 'gdp_time_series.png' file successfully created in the target directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Gretl GUI to create the plot
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Gretl's graphical interface (menus, right-clicks, graph windows) rather than using terminal commands to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The plot was genuinely generated by Gretl
integrity.visual_evidence: The graph window displays standard Gretl charting UI elements (like its specific toolbar or window frame), proving it wasn't an image opened from the web or another program.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to fake the output file
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal commands (like 'touch', 'wget', 'cp', or python scripts) used to artificially create a file named 'gdp_time_series.png' without actually exporting it from Gretl.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset is 'usa.gdt' containing 103 observations from 1984Q1 to 2009Q3. The target variable is 'gdp' (Real Gross Domestic Product). The resulting plot must be a time-series graph with the x-axis spanning 1984 to 2009. The exact required output path is '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/gdp_time_series.png'.
